metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_554
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Localization of Munc13 isoforms at the calyx of Held synapse.
- Fig.1 shows localization of native protein with subtype-specific antibodies. Strong colocalization with the presynaptic marker VGlut1 together with a lack of staining of the postsynaptic MNTB cell body strongly support a presynaptic localization.
- Fig.2/3 shows localization of genetically encoded EGFP attached to native mouse Munc13 paralogs. Strong colocalization with the presynaptic markers VGlut1 and Bassoon together with a lack of staining of the postsynaptic MNTB cell body strongly support a presynaptic localization.

12/5/2017 Pim:
- Authors state to distinguish pre- from postsynaptic localization. Literal: "First, triple labeling was performed for GFP, the somatic protein MAP-2, and the presynaptic protein VGlut1 in auditory brainstem slices of each strain (Fig. 2). MAP-2 staining allowed one to distinguish calyceal from somatic labeling in Munc13-2-EYFP and Munc13-3-EGFP MNTB neurons (Figs. 2, 3)... Munc13-1-EYFP, Munc13-2-EYFP, and Munc13-3-EGFP signals were clearly associated with VGluT1-labeled presynaptic specializations, essentially corresponding to calyx of Held terminals (Fig. 2)....". Experimental description: Figure 1 shows expression of Munc13-2 in mouse clay of Held synapses. As a negative control for the antibody staining calyces from Munc13-2 KO mice were used. The mice have been described previously (Augustin et al., 2001; Varoqueaux et al., 2002).
Fig.2 and Fig.3 show localization of genetically encoded EGFP attached to native mouse Munc13 paralogs. Munc13-XFP mice have been described previously (Kalla et al., 2006; Cooper et al., 2012)
